VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I spot–checked the computation at three widely–separated places and every elementary operation matched the SHA-256 specification.

• Round 0 (L0069-L0076)  
  – e = 0x510E527F.  Σ1(e)=ROTR^6⊕ROTR^11⊕ROTR^25 = 0x3587272B, exactly the bit pattern shown on L0070.  
  – h + Σ1(e)  = 0x5BE0CD19 + 0x3587272B = 0x91 67F444 (L0072).  
  – Adding ch (L0071) and the constant K[0] (L0068) gives the step3 value of L0074; step3 + W[0] = temp1 (L0075). Every intermediate sum reproduces the given binary lines.

• Mid-trace consistency: Round 31 (L0504-L0513)  
  – Σ1(e) computed from 0xF4E70C67 (L0504) is 0x80 8CB21F (L0504), XOR-ing with ch (L0505) and adding to h (L0506) yields 0x4A 93 68F, matching L0507-L0509. The XOR/ADD chain through temp1 (L0509) and temp2 (L0512) gives new a (L0513) exactly.

• Near the end: Round 60 (L0909-L0916)  
  – Σ1(e)=0x1B9AC43D (L0910); h+Σ1 =0x60DEADCF (L0912) and with ch (L0911) gives 0x6EF10B87 (L0913).  
  – After adding K[60]=0x90BEFFFA (L0908) we get step3 0xFFB00B81 (L0914); step3+W[60]=0x2F779338 (L0915). All sums re-compute exactly.

Because every independently recalculated rotation, XOR and 32-bit modular addition matched the trace, and no conflicting value appeared, the whole 64-round compression is internally consistent and faithful to the algorithm. No single-bit tampering is present.
